quizQuestion: Which media franchise is the highest-grossing of all time with over $147 billion in total revenue?
explanation: Pokémon has generated over $147 billion since its creation in 1996, surpassing Hello Kitty, Star Wars, and every other media franchise through video games, trading cards, merchandise, anime, and more. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

quizQuestion: Who created Pokémon in 1996, partly inspired by his childhood hobby of collecting insects?
explanation: Satoshi Tajiri, along with Ken Sugimori, created Pokémon for Game Freak / Nintendo. Tajiri's childhood love of insect collecting in the countryside directly inspired the concept of catching and collecting creatures. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

quizQuestion: In what year was the Pokémon franchise first launched?
explanation: Pokémon debuted in 1996 with the release of Pocket Monsters Red and Green for the Game Boy in Japan. The franchise has since grown to include anime, trading cards, movies, merchandise, and apps like Pokémon GO. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

quizQuestion: Which designer created the iconic Hello Kitty character for Sanrio in 1974?
explanation: Yuko Shimizu designed Hello Kitty for the Japanese company Sanrio in 1974. The character has since become one of the most recognizable brand icons globally, appearing on over 50,000 products in more than 130 countries. Source: Wikipedia/Hello_Kitty

quizQuestion: Which Japanese company owns the Hello Kitty brand and produces over 50,000 products worldwide?
explanation: Sanrio is the Japanese company that owns Hello Kitty, created in 1974. The brand generates over $80 billion in total revenue and operates in more than 130 countries with tens of thousands of licensed products. Source: Wikipedia/Hello_Kitty

quizQuestion: Approximately how many different products feature the Hello Kitty brand globally?
explanation: Hello Kitty appears on over 50,000 products sold in more than 130 countries, making it one of the most extensively merchandised characters in history. The character generates over $80 billion in total revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Hello_Kitty

quizQuestion: Which filmmaker created the Star Wars universe, first releasing it to cinemas in 1977?
explanation: George Lucas created Star Wars, which debuted in 1977 and became one of the most financially successful franchises in history, generating over $65 billion. Lucas later sold the franchise to The Walt Disney Company in 2012. Source: Wikipedia/Star_Wars

quizQuestion: In what year did the original Star Wars film first hit theaters, launching one of the biggest franchises in history?
explanation: Star Wars: Episode IV — A New Hope was released in 1977, created by George Lucas. It launched a franchise that has since generated over $65 billion across films, merchandise, theme parks, and expanded universe media. Source: Wikipedia/Star_Wars

quizQuestion: Which British author created the Harry Potter series, first publishing the debut novel in 1997?
explanation: J.K. Rowling created the Harry Potter series, beginning with Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone in 1997. The franchise has sold over 500 million books and generated $25–43 billion across books, films, merchandise, and theme parks. Source: Wikipedia/Harry_Potter

quizQuestion: Approximately how many millions of copies of Harry Potter books have been sold worldwide?
explanation: The Harry Potter book series has sold over 500 million copies worldwide, making it one of the best-selling book series in history. Combined with 8 films grossing $8.5 billion, the franchise total reaches $25–43 billion. Source: Wikipedia/Harry_Potter

quizQuestion: How many billion dollars did the 8 mainline Harry Potter films gross at the global box office?
explanation: The 8 Harry Potter films grossed approximately $8.5 billion at the worldwide box office, making it one of the most successful film series of all time. The wider franchise including books, merchandise, and theme parks generates $25–43 billion in total. Source: Wikipedia/Harry_Potter

quizQuestion: Which 2008 superhero film launched the Marvel Cinematic Universe franchise?
explanation: Iron Man (2008), directed by Jon Favreau and starring Robert Downey Jr., was the first film in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. The MCU has since become the highest-grossing film franchise of all time with over $29 billion at the global box office. Source: Wikipedia/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe

quizQuestion: In what year did the Marvel Cinematic Universe officially begin with the release of Iron Man?
explanation: The Marvel Cinematic Universe launched in 2008 with Iron Man. Over the following 15+ years, the MCU expanded to 30+ films, becoming the highest-grossing film franchise of all time with over $29 billion in worldwide box office revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe

quizQuestion: Which film franchise holds the record as the highest-grossing of all time with over $29 billion at the box office?
explanation: The Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) is the highest-grossing film franchise ever, surpassing the James Bond, Star Wars, and Harry Potter series. It launched in 2008 with Iron Man and has produced 30+ films. Source: Wikipedia/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe

quizQuestion: Which Nintendo designer created Mario, originally because the company couldn't license the Popeye character?
explanation: Shigeru Miyamoto created Mario after Nintendo failed to acquire the Popeye license for an arcade game. The Mario franchise has since become the best-selling video game franchise of all time, with hundreds of millions of games sold. Source: Wikipedia/Mario

quizQuestion: Which Nintendo franchise holds the title of the best-selling video game series of all time?
explanation: The Mario franchise is the best-selling video game franchise in history. Originally created by Shigeru Miyamoto after Nintendo couldn't license Popeye, Mario has appeared in hundreds of games across every Nintendo platform since the 1980s. Source: Wikipedia/Mario

quizQuestion: In what year did the Call of Duty franchise launch its first game?
explanation: Call of Duty debuted in 2003, developed by Infinity Ward and published by Activision. The franchise has since sold over 500 million copies and generated over $30 billion in lifetime revenue, making it one of gaming's most commercially successful series. Source: Wikipedia/Call_of_Duty

quizQuestion: Which game studio originally developed the first Call of Duty game in 2003?
explanation: Infinity Ward developed the original Call of Duty in 2003 in partnership with Activision. The franchise has grown to include multiple studios and has sold over 500 million copies, generating more than $30 billion in lifetime revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Call_of_Duty

quizQuestion: Approximately how many millions of copies has the Call of Duty franchise sold across all its titles?
explanation: Call of Duty has sold over 500 million copies since its 2003 debut, generating more than $30 billion in lifetime revenue. It consistently ranks as one of the top-selling game franchises alongside Mario and Pokémon. Source: Wikipedia/Call_of_Duty

quizQuestion: In what year did the Grand Theft Auto franchise begin with its first game?
explanation: Grand Theft Auto debuted in 1997, developed by Rockstar Games. The franchise has since sold over 460 million copies, with GTA V alone generating over $10.22 billion, making it one of the highest-grossing entertainment products in history. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto

quizQuestion: Which game studio developed the Grand Theft Auto franchise?
explanation: Rockstar Games developed the Grand Theft Auto franchise, which debuted in 1997. The series has sold over 460 million copies and GTA V alone generated over $10.22 billion, making it one of the most profitable entertainment franchises ever. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto

quizQuestion: Approximately how many millions of copies has the entire Grand Theft Auto franchise sold?
explanation: The Grand Theft Auto franchise has sold over 460 million copies. GTA V alone generated $10.22 billion+ since its 2013 release, making it the highest-grossing entertainment product of its time. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto

quizQuestion: Who founded the LEGO company in Denmark in 1932?
explanation: Ole Kirk Christiansen founded the LEGO company in Billund, Denmark in 1932. The name LEGO comes from the Danish phrase 'leg godt,' meaning 'play well.' The company has produced over 6 billion bricks since 1968. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

quizQuestion: In what year was the LEGO company founded in Denmark?
explanation: LEGO was founded in 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen in Billund, Denmark. The name comes from the Danish 'leg godt' meaning 'play well.' The company has become one of the world's most recognized toy brands, producing over 6 billion bricks since 1968. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

quizQuestion: The name LEGO comes from which Danish phrase meaning 'play well'?
explanation: LEGO is derived from the Danish phrase 'leg godt,' meaning 'play well.' Founded in 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen in Denmark, the LEGO company has since become one of the world's most iconic toy brands, producing over 6 billion bricks since 1968. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

quizQuestion: Approximately how many billion LEGO bricks have been produced since the company began making them in 1968?
explanation: LEGO has produced over 6 billion bricks since 1968, when the iconic interlocking plastic brick design was standardized. The company is based in Billund, Denmark, and was founded in 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

quizQuestion: Which co-founder of Mattel created Hot Wheels in 1968?
explanation: Elliot Handler, co-founder of Mattel, created Hot Wheels in 1968. The brand has since manufactured over 6 billion die-cast cars and generated $1.43 billion in revenue in 2023 alone, making it the world's best-selling toy vehicle brand. Source: Wikipedia/Hot_Wheels

quizQuestion: In what year did Hot Wheels launch, creating the world's best-selling toy vehicle brand?
explanation: Hot Wheels launched in 1968, created by Elliot Handler of Mattel. The brand has since produced over 6 billion die-cast cars and generates over $1 billion in annual revenue, making it the dominant name in toy vehicles globally. Source: Wikipedia/Hot_Wheels

quizQuestion: Approximately how many billion Hot Wheels cars have been manufactured since the brand launched?
explanation: Hot Wheels has manufactured over 6 billion die-cast cars since its 1968 debut, making it the world's best-selling toy vehicle brand. Mattel generated $1.43 billion in Hot Wheels revenue in 2023. Source: Wikipedia/Hot_Wheels

quizQuestion: In what year did Mattel launch the Barbie doll at the New York Toy Fair?
explanation: Barbie debuted at the New York Toy Fair in 1959, created by Ruth Handler. The doll became one of the most iconic toys in history, generating $1.54 billion in revenue for Mattel in 2023, boosted further by the blockbuster 2023 film. Source: Wikipedia/Barbie

quizQuestion: Which toy company owns and produces the Barbie doll brand?
explanation: Mattel owns Barbie, which debuted in 1959 and has remained one of the world's best-selling toy lines. The brand generated $1.54 billion in 2023 revenue, receiving additional attention from the major 2023 blockbuster film. Source: Wikipedia/Barbie

quizQuestion: In what year did the Transformers franchise debut with its toy line and animated series?
explanation: The Transformers franchise launched in 1984, developed by Hasbro. It has since generated over $25 billion across toys, animated series, comics, and the Michael Bay film series, making it one of Hasbro's most valuable properties. Source: Wikipedia/Transformers

quizQuestion: Which toy company owns the Transformers franchise that debuted in 1984?
explanation: Hasbro owns Transformers, which launched in 1984. The franchise has generated over $25 billion total across toys, animated series, comics, and the popular Michael Bay film adaptations. Source: Wikipedia/Transformers

quizQuestion: Which Hasbro franchise, featuring robots that disguise themselves as vehicles, has generated over $25 billion since launching in 1984?
explanation: Transformers has generated over $25 billion since debuting in 1984. The franchise spans toys, animated TV shows, comics, and blockbuster films. It remains one of Hasbro's flagship properties. Source: Wikipedia/Transformers

quizQuestion: Which video game development studio, working with Nintendo, created the original Pokémon games?
explanation: Game Freak developed the original Pokémon games in collaboration with Nintendo. Founded by Satoshi Tajiri and Ken Sugimori in 1996, the franchise became the highest-grossing media franchise of all time with over $147 billion in total revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon
